Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class AlternativeOrder

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.AlternativeOrder
public class AlternativeOrder extends Object
AlternativeOrder

An order for an alternative investment.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlternativeOrder

      public AlternativeOrder(Optional<String> accountId, Optional<String> alternativeOrderId, Optional<String> clientOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue, Optional<String> currencyCode, Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderDistributionType> distributionType, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderFilledQuantity> filledQuantity, Optional<Boolean> full, Optional<String> identifier, Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderIdentifierType> identifierType, Optional<String> name, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderNotionalValue> notionalValue, Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderOrderStatus> orderStatus, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderQuantity> quantity, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends SettlementDate> settlementDate, Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderSide> side, Optional<String> statusMessage, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> updateTime)

    • AlternativeOrder

      public AlternativeOrder()

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      The ID of the account.

    • alternativeOrderId

      public Optional<String> alternativeOrderId()
      The ID of the alternative investment order.

    • clientOrderId

      public Optional<String> clientOrderId()
      A client-supplied unique ID to enforce idempotency. Each ID must be unique within the account.

      If a create request includes a `client_order_id` that matches an existing order’s `client_order_id`, the service will respond with `ALREADY_EXISTS` and will not create a new order resource.

    • createTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime()
      The date and time the order was created. Uses [ISO 8601 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AlternativeOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue()
      The actual amount of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `notional_value`.

    • currencyCode

      public Optional<String> currencyCode()
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • distributionType

      public Optional<AlternativeOrderDistributionType> distributionType()
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • filledQuantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AlternativeOrderFilledQuantity> filledQuantity()
      The actual filled share quantity of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `quantity`.

    • full

      public Optional<Boolean> full()
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • identifier

      public Optional<String> identifier()
      The asset identifier value, dependent on enum value set for `identifier_type`.

      For example, if `identifier_type` = `ASSET_ID`, then the value for `identifier` is the `asset_id` value.

    • identifierType

      public Optional<AlternativeOrderIdentifierType> identifierType()
      Identifies which parameter value is used to identify the asset in the order. The value for that parameter is used in the `identifier` field.

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment order.

      Format: accounts/{account}/alternativeOrders/{alternative_order}

      Note that `{account}` will be the `account_id` value and `{alternative_order}` will be the `alternative_order_id` value.

    • notionalValue

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AlternativeOrderNotionalValue> notionalValue()
      The requested notional value for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - The value must be positive. - Values are scaled to two decimal places. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested amount.

    • orderStatus

      public Optional<AlternativeOrderOrderStatus> orderStatus()
      The order’s status at the time of the response.

    • quantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AlternativeOrderQuantity> quantity()
      The requested quantity of shares for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Only relevant to redemption orders. - The value must be positive. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested quantity.

    • settlementDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<SettlementDate> settlementDate()
      The date the order will be/was settled. Value will be `null` until the issuer sets a settlement date.

    • side

      public Optional<AlternativeOrderSide> side()
      The side of the order.

    • statusMessage

      public Optional<String> statusMessage()
      A system-generated, user-friendly message about the order’s status.

    • updateTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> updateTime()
      The date and time the order was last updated. Uses [ISO 8601 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

    • builder

      public static AlternativeOrder.Builder builder()

    • withAccountId

      public AlternativeOrder withAccountId(String accountId)
      The ID of the account.

    • withAccountId

      public AlternativeOrder withAccountId(Optional<String> accountId)
      The ID of the account.

    • withAlternativeOrderId

      public AlternativeOrder withAlternativeOrderId(String alternativeOrderId)
      The ID of the alternative investment order.

    • withAlternativeOrderId

      public AlternativeOrder withAlternativeOrderId(Optional<String> alternativeOrderId)
      The ID of the alternative investment order.

    • withClientOrderId

      public AlternativeOrder withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      A client-supplied unique ID to enforce idempotency. Each ID must be unique within the account.

      If a create request includes a `client_order_id` that matches an existing order’s `client_order_id`, the service will respond with `ALREADY_EXISTS` and will not create a new order resource.

    • withClientOrderId

      public AlternativeOrder withClientOrderId(Optional<String> clientOrderId)
      A client-supplied unique ID to enforce idempotency. Each ID must be unique within the account.

      If a create request includes a `client_order_id` that matches an existing order’s `client_order_id`, the service will respond with `ALREADY_EXISTS` and will not create a new order resource.

    • withCreateTime

      public AlternativeOrder withCreateTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
      The date and time the order was created. Uses [ISO 8601 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

    • withCreateTime

      public AlternativeOrder withCreateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime)
      The date and time the order was created. Uses [ISO 8601 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrder withCumulativeNotionalValue(AlternativeOrderCumulativeNotionalValue cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The actual amount of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `notional_value`.

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrder withCumulativeNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The actual amount of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `notional_value`.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public AlternativeOrder withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public AlternativeOrder withCurrencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • withDistributionType

      public AlternativeOrder withDistributionType(AlternativeOrderDistributionType distributionType)
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • withDistributionType

      public AlternativeOrder withDistributionType(Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderDistributionType> distributionType)
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • withFilledQuantity

      public AlternativeOrder withFilledQuantity(AlternativeOrderFilledQuantity filledQuantity)
      The actual filled share quantity of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `quantity`.

    • withFilledQuantity

      public AlternativeOrder withFilledQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderFilledQuantity> filledQuantity)
      The actual filled share quantity of the order, which is determined after the issuer accepts the order. May differ from the requested `quantity`.

    • withFull

      public AlternativeOrder withFull(boolean full)
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • withFull

      public AlternativeOrder withFull(Optional<Boolean> full)
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • withIdentifier

      public AlternativeOrder withIdentifier(String identifier)
      The asset identifier value, dependent on enum value set for `identifier_type`.

      For example, if `identifier_type` = `ASSET_ID`, then the value for `identifier` is the `asset_id` value.

    • withIdentifier

      public AlternativeOrder withIdentifier(Optional<String> identifier)
      The asset identifier value, dependent on enum value set for `identifier_type`.

      For example, if `identifier_type` = `ASSET_ID`, then the value for `identifier` is the `asset_id` value.

    • withIdentifierType

      public AlternativeOrder withIdentifierType(AlternativeOrderIdentifierType identifierType)
      Identifies which parameter value is used to identify the asset in the order. The value for that parameter is used in the `identifier` field.

    • withIdentifierType

      public AlternativeOrder withIdentifierType(Optional<? extends AlternativeOrderIdentifierType> identifierType)
      Identifies which parameter value is used to identify the asset in the order. The value for that parameter is used in the `identifier` field.

    • withName

      public AlternativeOrder withName(String name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment order.

      Format: accounts/{account}/alternativeOrders/{alternative_order}

      Note that `{account}` will be the `account_id` value and `{alternative_order}` will be the `alternative_order_id` value.

    • withName

      public AlternativeOrder withName(Optional<String> name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment order.

      Format: accounts/{account}/alternativeOrders/{alternative_order}

      Note that `{account}` will be the `account_id` value and `{alternative_order}` will be the `alternative_order_id` value.

    • withNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrder withNotionalValue(AlternativeOrderNotionalValue notionalValue)
      The requested notional value for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - The value must be positive. - Values are scaled to two decimal places. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested amount.

    • withNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrder withNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AlternativeOrderNotionalValue> notionalValue)
      The requested notional value for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - The value must be positive. - Values are scaled to two decimal places. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested amount.

      The order’s status at the time of the response.

    • withQuantity

      public AlternativeOrder withQuantity(AlternativeOrderQuantity quantity)
      The requested quantity of shares for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Only relevant to redemption orders. - The value must be positive. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested quantity.

      The requested quantity of shares for the order. - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Only relevant to redemption orders. - The value must be positive. - Note that the issuer may allow only an order for less than the requested quantity.

      The date the order will be/was settled. Value will be `null` until the issuer sets a settlement date.
